response.justification: The candidate is an experienced, versatile senior engineer with strong web/full-stack and some systems-level background, but his profile is a poor match for this role's core requirements. His professional C++ experience is limited to a seven-month stint at National Instruments in 2014–2015; his systems work since then has been in DLang and Node.js/TypeScript. He shows no evidence of IEC 62443/SDL practice, MQTT, message-broker architectures at industrial scale, or the industrial-automation protocol stack the role treats as core or advantageous. Retraining a strong generalist is possible, but for a senior-manager-level C++ position requiring 8–12 years in the domain, the gap is too large.
strengths: 15+ years of broad software engineering experience across web, mobile, desktop, and embedded systems
Solid engineering-craft credentials: TDD, CI/CD, testing tools authorship, mentoring, workshops, and conference talks
Low-latency, high-throughput systems experience (millions of HTTP req/s at Dunnhumby, async I/O, event-driven Geo-Data platform)
concerns: Minimal professional C++ experience (roughly 7 months in 2014–2015); recent work is TypeScript/Node.js/DLang
No demonstrated IEC 62443, Secure Development Lifecycle, or industrial-cybersecurity experience
No exposure to MQTT, Modbus, OPC UA, or other industrial-automation protocols and platforms

key_factors.factor: Strong general seniority and distributed/low-latency systems skills
